title: [lichen_util.h:93] cast through uintptr_t may cause alignment issues on strict platforms
description: At line 93, the code casts through uintptr_t to get a word-aligned pointer: 'volatile unsigned long *wp = (volatile unsigned long *)(uintptr_t)p'. While p is aligned at this point due to the loop at lines 87-90, the cast through uintptr_t loses the volatile qualifier temporarily. The final result is volatile, but intermediate casts could theoretically allow optimization. More concerning: if sizeof(unsigned long) is 8 on 64-bit but the alignment loop only aligns to 4 bytes (if uintptr_t alignment differs), this could cause unaligned access. The code assumes sizeof(unsigned long) == alignment requirement, which is typically true but not guaranteed.
close_reason: Fixed: Added detailed comment documenting alignment correctness, changed to uint32_t for consistent 4-byte alignment
